To avoid over‑packing that would worsen surface tension, we also raise rmin slightly. Raising rmin expands the LJ core, reducing excessive packing and therefore tempering the surface‑tension excess caused by stronger attraction. Increasing the bond force constant (kb) keeps the longer bond geometry stable.
